_comment: Single source of truth for Composite Emission Score (CES) weights and BS-IV/BS-VI thresholds. Edited by hand; consumed by backend/emission_engine.py via ces_constants.py (auto-generated) AND by scripts/gen_ces_consts.py which cross-checks the Solidity integer constants in contracts/EmissionRegistry.sol. DO NOT edit this file without running `python scripts/gen_ces_consts.py --check` afterwards.

disclosure.ces_weights_origin: Author-proposed health-weighted composite scheme. Not a regulatory standard. ARAI/MoRTH do not specify a multi-pollutant composite score for BS-VI.
disclosure.solidity_scaling_note: Pollutants and thresholds are scaled x1000 on-chain. CES scores and weights are scaled x10000 on-chain. The JSON carries both float and integer representations so the generator can cross-check.

rationale.co2: Largest contributor to climate impact; dominant by mass in tailpipe emissions.
rationale.nox: NOx contributes more to tropospheric ozone formation and has the strictest BSVI limit (0.06 g/km vs 1.0 g/km for CO). Weighting reflects health-impact severity per WHO Air Quality Guidelines 2021.
rationale.co: Significant health hazard (carboxyhemoglobin formation) but higher absolute threshold allows more tolerance.
rationale.hc: Precursor to ground-level ozone; moderate direct health impact.
rationale.pm25: Highly toxic per unit mass (penetrates deep into lungs) but emitted in very small quantities by modern petrol engines with particulate filters.
